title: Retrieval-Augmented Reasoning for Scientific Claim Verification
abstract: We study whether retrieval-augmented pipelines improve scientific claim verification. We compare dense retrieval, hybrid retrieval, and a reasoning-augmented verifier across three benchmark datasets and show that retrieval quality strongly constrains downstream verification accuracy.
annotation_text: Claim verification improves when the retriever is tuned on in-domain scientific text. The reasoning stage reduces false positives but remains sensitive to incomplete evidence. Authors note limited generalization to low-resource domains.

annotations.text: Hybrid retrieval outperformed dense retrieval on all three datasets.
annotations.comment: Important for comparing baseline design choices.

annotations.text: The verifier reduced false positives by 11.2% relative to the retrieval-only baseline.
annotations.comment: Likely useful for the findings section.
